tools.name: create_blueprint_actor
tools.description:
Create a new Blueprint asset with optional inline components and variables. Compiles and saves automatically.

Common parent_class values: Actor, Pawn, Character, PlayerController, GameModeBase, HUD, GameStateBase, PlayerState, ActorComponent, SceneComponent.

For a 3rd-person character use parent_class='Character' with SpringArmComponent (CameraBoom) + CameraComponent (FollowCamera).
For a projectile use parent_class='Actor' with ProjectileMovementComponent + SphereComponent + StaticMeshComponent.

After creation, use inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d to add logic, and set_component_properties to assign meshes.

asset_path.description: Content path for the new Blueprint, e.g. /Game/Blueprints/BP_Enemy

parent_class.description: Parent class name. Aliases: Actor, Pawn, Character, PlayerController, GameModeBase. Or any UE class name.

components.description: Components to add via the Simple Construction Script (SCS). Each becomes a variable accessible from the Event Graph.

name.description: Variable name for the component in the Blueprint, e.g. CameraBoom, FollowCamera, CollisionBox

class.description: Component class name (without the U prefix). Examples: StaticMeshComponent, SkeletalMeshComponent, CapsuleComponent, BoxComponent, SphereComponent, SpringArmComponent, CameraComponent, CharacterMovementComponent, FloatingPawnMovement, ProjectileMovementComponent, ArrowComponent, AudioComponent, NiagaraComponent, WidgetComponent, SceneComponent, PointLightComponent, SpotLightComponent, RectLightComponent

attach_to.description: Variable name of the parent component to attach to. Use the name of another component in this array, or 'DefaultSceneRoot' for the actor root. If omitted, attaches to the default scene root.

variables.description: Member variables to add to the Blueprint.

type.description: Variable type: bool, int, int64, float, double, FString, FText, FName, byte, FVector, FRotator, FTransform, FLinearColor, FVector2D, or any UClass/UStruct name

editable.description: Whether editable in the Details panel on level instances (default: true)

category.description: Category for organizing in the Details panel

tools.name: add_blueprint_component
tools.description: Add a component to an existing Blueprint via the Simple Construction Script. The component becomes a variable accessible from the Event Graph by its component_name.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint to modify

component_class.description: Component class name (without U prefix): StaticMeshComponent, SkeletalMeshComponent, BoxComponent, SphereComponent, CapsuleComponent, SpringArmComponent, CameraComponent, ProjectileMovementComponent, etc.

component_name.description: Variable name for the new component in the Blueprint, e.g. CameraBoom, HealthBar, TriggerBox

attach_to.description: Variable name of the parent component to attach to. Use get_blueprint_info to find existing component names.

tools.name: add_blueprint_variable
tools.description: Add a typed member variable to an existing Blueprint. Variables can then be read/written in the Event Graph via VariableGet/VariableSet nodes in T3D.

variable_name.description: Name for the new variable, e.g. Health, MaxSpeed, bIsAlive

variable_type.description: Type: bool, int, int64, float, double, FString, FText, FName, byte, FVector, FRotator, FTransform, FLinearColor, or any class/struct name

editable.description: Whether editable in the Details panel (default: true)

category.description: Category for grouping in Details panel, e.g. 'Combat', 'Movement'

tools.name: add_blueprint_function
tools.description:
Add a new named function graph to a Blueprint. Creates the function entry and result nodes automatically.

After creation, populate the function body using inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d with graph_name equal to this function_name.

IMPORTANT: Latent nodes (Delay, MoveComponentTo, AIMoveTo) CANNOT be used inside functions — use Custom Events in the EventGraph instead.

function_name.description: Name for the new function, e.g. CalculateDamage, ApplyHealthChange, GetNearestEnemy

inputs.description: Input parameter definitions for the function signature

type.description: Parameter type: bool, int, float, FVector, etc.

outputs.description: Return value definitions for the function signature

tools.name: add_blueprint_event
tools.description:
Add a standard override event node to the Blueprint EventGraph. Avoids duplicate event nodes.

Supported event_name values:
  Actor: BeginPlay, EndPlay, Tick, ActorBeginOverlap, ActorEndOverlap, Hit, AnyDamage, PointDamage, RadialDamage, Destroyed, ActorBeginCursorOver, ActorEndCursorOver
  Pawn: PossessedBy, UnPossessed, SetupPlayerInputComponent
  Character: Landed

NOTE on Enhanced Input: For player input handling with the Enhanced Input System, use SetupPlayerInputComponent to bind IA_* Input Action assets to Blueprint functions. Do NOT use old Input Axis/Action events — they are legacy.

RETURNS: The internal node name (e.g. "K2Node_Event_0"), position, and the exec output pin name. Use the returned node name with connect_blueprint_pins to wire nodes created in separate calls.

event_name.description: One of: BeginPlay, EndPlay, Tick, ActorBeginOverlap, ActorEndOverlap, Hit, AnyDamage, PointDamage, RadialDamage, Destroyed, ActorBeginCursorOver, ActorEndCursorOver, PossessedBy, UnPossessed, SetupPlayerInputComponent, Landed

node_pos_x.description: Horizontal position of the event node in the graph (default: 0)

node_pos_y.description: Vertical position hint for the event node. Default: auto (placed below existing nodes).

tools.name: compile_blueprint
tools.description: Compile a Blueprint and return all errors and warnings. Use this to verify correctness after node injection or other modifications.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint to compile

tools.name: set_blueprint_defaults
tools.description:
Set default property values on a Blueprint's Class Default Object (CDO).

For component properties, use the 'ComponentName.PropertyName' syntax.

Examples:
  { "TargetArmLength": "300" }  — direct CDO property
  { "CameraBoom.TargetArmLength": "300" }  — component template property
  { "DefaultPawnClass": "/Game/Blueprints/BP_MyCharacter.BP_MyCharacter_C" }  — object reference

Property names are exact C++ property names (case-sensitive). Use get_blueprint_info to see available variable names.
PREFER set_component_properties for mesh assignment, transforms, and collision — it has dedicated first-class support.

defaults.description: Key-value pairs of property names and their string-serialized values. Use 'ComponentName.PropertyName' for component properties.

tools.name: set_component_properties
tools.description:
Set properties on a specific SCS component template inside a Blueprint. This is the CORRECT way to:
  - Assign a static mesh to a StaticMeshComponent
  - Assign a skeletal mesh to a SkeletalMeshComponent
  - Set relative location, rotation, or scale
  - Set a collision profile
  - Set any other component property via reflection

Use get_blueprint_info first to confirm the component variable name.

component_name.description: Variable name of the SCS component to modify (exactly as shown in get_blueprint_info)

static_mesh.description: Content path to a UStaticMesh asset to assign, e.g. /Game/Meshes/SM_Rock

skeletal_mesh.description: Content path to a USkeletalMesh asset to assign, e.g. /Game/Characters/SK_Mannequin

collision_profile.description: Collision preset name: NoCollision, BlockAll, BlockAllDynamic, OverlapAll, OverlapAllDynamic, Trigger, Pawn, CharacterMesh, PhysicsActor, Projectile, Custom

properties.description: Generic reflection-based property writes. Keys are exact C++ property names; values are string-serialized. Example: {"TargetArmLength": "400", "bUsePawnControlRotation": "true"}

tools.name: inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d
tools.description:
PRIMARY LOGIC GRAPH CONSTRUCTION METHOD.

Injects T3D-serialized Blueprint node text directly into a Blueprint graph. Internally uses FEdGraphUtilities::ImportNodesFromText — the exact same mechanism as Blueprint editor paste (Ctrl+V).

This is the preferred approach for all logic construction because:
  1. T3D is text — LLMs generate it far more accurately than navigating the C++ FGraphNodeCreator API
  2. Entire systems (health logic, movement state machines) can be built in one block
  3. Malformed T3D fails silently rather than crashing the editor

=== T3D FORMAT RULES ===

Each node block:
  Begin Object Class=/Script/BlueprintGraph.K2Node_XXX Name="UniqueNodeName"
    NodePosX=100
    NodePosY=200
    CustomProperties Pin (PinId=LINK_1,PinName="execute",PinType.PinCategory="exec")
    CustomProperties Pin (PinId=LINK_2,PinName="result",Direction=EGPD_Output,PinType.PinCategory="exec",LinkedTo=(UniqueOtherNode LINK_3))
  End Object

GUID Placeholders:
  Use LINK_1, LINK_2, GUID_A, NODEREF_Entry etc. as placeholder IDs.
  The plugin auto-resolves them to fresh GUIDs before injection.
  Each unique placeholder gets the SAME real GUID throughout the T3D block, so cross-node links are preserved.

Pin Directions:
  Input pins: no Direction field (default = EGPD_Input)
  Output pins: Direction=EGPD_Output

Pin Categories:
  exec, bool, int, int64, real (float/double), name, string, text, byte, object, struct

Linking pins across nodes:
  LinkedTo=(OtherNodeName OtherPinId)

=== COMMON NODE CLASSES ===

Control Flow:
  UK2Node_IfThenElse           — Branch (Condition bool input, True/False exec outputs)
  UK2Node_ExecutionSequence    — Sequence (Then_0, Then_1, Then_2... exec outputs)
  UK2Node_MacroInstance        — ForEachLoop, WhileLoop (uses standard macro library)
  UK2Node_FunctionResult       — Return Node (for function graphs only)
  UK2Node_CustomEvent          — Custom Event (use in EventGraph; supports latent nodes)

Variable Access:
  UK2Node_VariableGet          — Get a Blueprint variable
  UK2Node_VariableSet          — Set a Blueprint variable

Function Calls:
  UK2Node_CallFunction with FunctionReference=(MemberParent="/Script/CoreUObject.Class'/Script/Engine.XXX'",MemberName="FunctionName")

Common Function Nodes:
  Print String:        MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetSystemLibrary', MemberName='PrintString'
  Delay:              MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetSystemLibrary', MemberName='Delay' (latent — EventGraph only)
  SetActorLocation:   MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='K2_SetActorLocation'
  GetActorLocation:   MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='K2_GetActorLocation'
  SetActorRotation:   MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='K2_SetActorRotation'
  GetActorRotation:   MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='K2_GetActorRotation'
  GetOverlappingActors: MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='GetOverlappingActors'
  SetHiddenInGame:    MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='SetActorHiddenInGame'
  DestroyActor:       MemberParent='/Script/Engine.Actor', MemberName='K2_DestroyActor'
  SpawnActor:         MemberParent='/Script/Engine.GameplayStatics', MemberName='BeginSpawningActorFromClass'
  GetPlayerPawn:      MemberParent='/Script/Engine.GameplayStatics', MemberName='GetPlayerPawn'
  GetPlayerController: MemberParent='/Script/Engine.GameplayStatics', MemberName='GetPlayerController'
  CastTo:             UK2Node_DynamicCast

Math (Pure — no exec pins):
  Add (float):        MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='Add_FloatFloat'
  Subtract (float):   MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='Subtract_FloatFloat'
  Multiply (float):   MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='Multiply_FloatFloat'
  Clamp (float):      MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='FClamp'
  Max (float):        MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='FMax'
  Min (float):        MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='FMin'
  InterpTo:           MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='FInterpTo'
  RandomFloat:        MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='RandomFloatInRange'
  VectorLength:       MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='VSize'
  VectorAdd:          MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='Add_VectorVector'
  MakeVector:         MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='MakeVector'
  FindLookAtRotation: MemberParent='/Script/Engine.KismetMathLibrary', MemberName='FindLookAtRotation'

=== SPATIAL LAYOUT RULES ===

Flow left-to-right (increasing NodePosX).
Vertical grouping: separate unrelated logic clusters by ~400 units on Y axis.
Leave ~300-400 units X between connected nodes.
First node after an event: NodePosX=300, same NodePosY as the event node.

=== POST-INJECTION PIN AUDIT ===

After every injection this tool automatically inspects all unconnected input pins on the imported nodes and appends a 'PINS REQUIRING ATTENTION' section to the result if problems are found.

Entry format:
  • [NodeTitle] pin "PinName" (category:SubType) = CurrentValue
    ACTION: Recommended action

Pin categories reported and what to do:
  object / softobject — DefaultObject is null (empty asset reference).
    → Call search_assets(class_filter='ClassName') BEFORE re-injecting to locate a valid asset.
    → Common examples: Material, StaticMesh, SkeletalMesh, AnimationAsset, Skeleton, PhysicsAsset, Sound.
    → After finding the asset, include its content path in the T3D DefaultObject field or use set_blueprint_defaults.
  real / float / int — value is 0 or empty.
    → Set an explicit non-zero value if the logic requires it.
    → Example: Make Color node for blue → R=0.0, G=0.0, B=1.0, A=1.0 (set DefaultValue on each pin).
  struct (FLinearColor, FVector, FRotator, etc.) — all components are zero.
    → Set explicit component values if a non-zero result is intended.
    → Example: blue FLinearColor → DefaultValue="(R=0.000000,G=0.000000,B=1.000000,A=1.000000)".

=== MANDATORY POST-INJECTION WORKFLOW ===
  1. After EVERY inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d call, read the entire result message.
  2. If a 'PINS REQUIRING ATTENTION' section is present, act on EVERY entry before considering the task done.
  3. For [EMPTY] object pins: call search_assets first, then assign the found asset path.
  4. For zero numeric/struct pins: supply the correct explicit value.
  5. Never leave asset references empty or leave numeric defaults at zero unless the task explicitly requires it.
  6. If you would have called Create Dynamic Material Instance, you MUST search for an available Material asset first.
  7. If you would set a color on Make Color or Make Linear Color, you MUST set all components explicitly (do not rely on defaults).

asset_path.description: Content path of the target Blueprint

t3d_text.description:
T3D-formatted node block(s). Each node: Begin Object Class=... Name="..."
  ...
End Object

graph_name.description: Name of the target graph. Default: 'EventGraph'. Use the function name to target a specific function graph. Use add_blueprint_function first if the graph doesn't exist yet.

tools.name: get_blueprint_info
tools.description:
Read-only query: returns a JSON description of a Blueprint's current state including compile status, parent class, all member variables (name/type/category), all SCS components (name/class/parent hierarchy), and all graphs with FULL NODE DETAILS (name, class, title, position, all pins with names/types/connections).

Call this BEFORE inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d to:
  - Know component variable names for T3D VariableGet nodes
  - Know which graphs already exist
  - Avoid creating duplicate variables or functions
  - Confirm the Blueprint compiled successfully

CALL THIS BEFORE connect_blueprint_pins to get the exact internal node names and pin names needed for wiring.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint to inspect

tools.name: connect_blueprint_pins
tools.description:
Explicitly wire two pins between existing nodes in a Blueprint graph.

Use this when nodes were added in separate tool calls and need to be connected. For example:
  1. add_blueprint_event returns node name 'K2Node_Event_0'
  2. inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d adds a PrintString node as 'K2Node_CallFunction_2'
  3. connect_blueprint_pins wires K2Node_Event_0.then -> K2Node_CallFunction_2.execute

WORKFLOW:
  1. Call get_blueprint_info to get the current internal node names and their pin names
  2. Call connect_blueprint_pins with the exact internal names
  3. If the connection is rejected, the tool returns the available pin names to help you pick the right one

COMMON PIN NAMES:
  Execution output: 'then' (most nodes), 'True'/'False' (Branch), 'Completed' (latent nodes)
  Execution input:  'execute' (most nodes)
  Data output:      'ReturnValue' (function returns), custom pin names
  Data input:       'Target' (self/object), 'Value', 'Condition', custom names

NOTE: The tool does type checking via the Blueprint schema. If types are incompatible it explains why.

graph_name.description: Graph to operate in. Default: 'EventGraph'. Use the function name for function graphs.

source_node.description: Internal name of the source node that has the output pin. Get this from get_blueprint_info or from the return value of add_blueprint_event. Example: 'K2Node_Event_0', 'K2Node_CallFunction_3'

source_pin.description: Output pin name on the source node. Common exec output pins: 'then', 'True', 'False', 'Completed'. Common data output pins: 'ReturnValue'. Get exact names from get_blueprint_info.

target_node.description: Internal name of the target node that has the input pin. Get this from get_blueprint_info. Example: 'K2Node_CallFunction_1', 'K2Node_VariableSet_0'

target_pin.description: Input pin name on the target node. Common exec input pins: 'execute'. Common data input pins: 'Target', 'Value', 'Condition', 'InDeltaTime'. Get exact names from get_blueprint_info.

tools.name: add_enhanced_input_node
tools.description:
Add a K2Node_EnhancedInputAction to a Blueprint's EventGraph.

This is REQUIRED for Enhanced Input — these nodes CANNOT be created via inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d because UInputAction asset references don't serialize through T3D text import.

After adding the node, use connect_blueprint_pins to wire its output pins (Triggered, Started, Completed, Ongoing, Canceled) to your handler function.

WORKFLOW:
1. Use search_assets to find the IA_ input action asset path
2. Call add_enhanced_input_node with the IA path
3. Use connect_blueprint_pins to wire 'Triggered' to your handler's 'execute' pin

Output pins on the created node:
  - Triggered (exec) — fires when input trigger conditions are met
  - Started (exec) — fires on initial press
  - Ongoing (exec) — fires every frame while held
  - Completed (exec) — fires on release after trigger
  - Canceled (exec) — fires if trigger conditions fail
  - ActionValue (struct) — the input value (bool/float/vector depending on action type)
  - ElapsedSeconds (float) — time since trigger started
  - TriggeredSeconds (float) — time since action was triggered

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint to add the node to (e.g. '/Game/ThirdPerson/Blueprints/BP_ThirdPersonCharacter.BP_ThirdPersonCharacter')

input_action.description: Content path of the UInputAction asset (e.g. '/Game/ThirdPerson/Input/Actions/IA_Jump')

tools.name: verify_blueprint_connections
tools.description:
Scan a Blueprint's graphs for connection issues and attempt automatic repairs.

USE THIS TOOL after inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d when the inject result contains a SANITISER warning (broken LinkedTo references), or when a compile step reports 'no connections on exec pin'.

PASS 1 — CollectIssues:
  - Removes stale/null LinkedTo references left behind by multi-batch T3D injections
  - Detects disconnected exec-output pins on FunctionEntry, CallFunction, and Event nodes
  - Detects disconnected exec-input pins on CallFunction and FunctionResult nodes

PASS 2 — AttemptRepair:
  - When a FunctionEntry exec-out is unconnected and exactly ONE CallFunction in the same graph has an unconnected exec-in, auto-wires them
  - Recompiles the Blueprint after any successful repair
  - Reports ambiguous cases (multiple candidates) as UNRESOLVED — use connect_blueprint_pins to wire those manually

OUTPUT: A structured report with three sections:
  [ISSUES FOUND]   — all detected problems
  [REPAIRS MADE]   — auto-wired connections
  [UNRESOLVED]     — issues requiring manual connect_blueprint_pins calls

WORKFLOW:
1. inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d → check for SANITISER warnings
2. If warnings present, or compile shows missing exec wires → call verify_blueprint_connections
3. For any UNRESOLVED entries → call connect_blueprint_pins with the node/pin names listed
4. compile_blueprint to confirm clean build

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint to verify (e.g. '/Game/Blueprints/BP_MyActor.BP_MyActor')

graph_name.description: Name of a specific graph to check (e.g. 'MyFunction', 'EventGraph'). If omitted, all UbergraphPages and FunctionGraphs are checked.

tools.name: set_node_pin_default
tools.description:
Set the default value of an INPUT pin on an existing Blueprint graph node.

This is the CORRECT tool for setting literal values on pins of already-existing nodes.
Use this when you need to set a value AFTER node injection or on pre-existing nodes.

DO NOT confuse with:
  - set_blueprint_defaults: sets CDO (Class Default Object) member variable defaults — NOT graph node pins
  - set_component_properties: sets SCS component template properties — NOT graph node pins
  - inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d: can set pin defaults at creation time via T3D DefaultValue, but CANNOT modify existing nodes

PIN TYPE FORMATS:
  bool:          "true" or "false"
  int:           "42"
  float/double:  "3.14"
  string:        Raw text (no quotes). Example: ThirdPersonMap
  name:          Raw text. Example: PlayerStart_0
  text (FText):  Raw text — automatically wrapped via FText::FromString
  enum:          Short entry name (e.g. "Automatic", not "EFireMode::Automatic")
  FVector:       "X=1.0 Y=2.0 Z=3.0"
  FRotator:      "P=0.0 Y=90.0 R=0.0"
  FLinearColor:  "(R=1.0,G=0.0,B=0.0,A=1.0)"
  FTransform:    "Translation=(X=0,Y=0,Z=0),Rotation=(X=0,Y=0,Z=0,W=1),Scale3D=(X=1,Y=1,Z=1)"
  object ref:    Asset path (e.g. "/Game/Meshes/SM_Rock.SM_Rock") — loaded via StaticLoadObject
  soft ref:      Asset path string (e.g. "/Game/Maps/ThirdPersonMap.ThirdPersonMap")

WORKFLOW:
1. Call get_blueprint_info to find the internal node name and pin names
2. Call set_node_pin_default with the exact node_name, pin_name, and formatted value
3. The tool validates the pin type, sets the value, and recompiles the Blueprint

EXAMPLES:
  Set a string literal:  { node_name: "K2Node_ConvName_0", pin_name: "InString", value: "ThirdPersonMap" }
  Set a level name:      { node_name: "K2Node_CallFunction_1", pin_name: "LevelName", value: "/Game/Maps/Level1" }
  Set a float:           { node_name: "K2Node_CallFunction_2", pin_name: "Duration", value: "2.5" }
  Set an enum:           { node_name: "K2Node_CallFunction_3", pin_name: "TeleportType", value: "None" }
  Set an object ref:     { node_name: "K2Node_CallFunction_4", pin_name: "SoundToPlay", value: "/Game/Audio/SFX_Click.SFX_Click" }

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint containing the node

node_name.description: Internal node name (e.g. 'K2Node_CallFunction_0', 'K2Node_ConvName_1'). Get this from get_blueprint_info.

pin_name.description: Input pin name on the node (e.g. 'InString', 'LevelName', 'Duration', 'Value'). Get this from get_blueprint_info.

value.description: The default value to set. Format depends on pin type — see tool description for format guide.

graph_name.description: Name of the graph containing the node. Default: 'EventGraph'. Use the function name for function graphs.
